AMD.headline: Data Center re-accelerating — +57% Q1 YoY vs +32% FY25; gross margin expanding; China export-control friction lowered but not gone.

shifts.trend: DC revenue $16.6B FY25 (+32% YoY) → $5.8B Q1 FY26 (+57% YoY); implies >$23B annual DC run rate
shifts.note: MI350 GPU and 5th-gen EPYC driving acceleration

shifts.trend: Gross margin 50% FY25 → 53% Q1 FY26 (+3pts); DC operating margin 22% FY25 → 28% Q1
shifts.note: Favorable mix shift to higher-margin DC as GPU revenue grows

shifts.trend: FY25: $800M MI308 inventory charge + $360M partial reversal; Feb 2026 MI325 licenses granted with 25% US re-import tariff — friction reduced but not eliminated
shifts.note: China remains 22% of FY25 revenue ($7.8B); Q1 not broken out

shifts.trend: No customer >10% in FY25 (vs one customer at 18% in FY23); international revenue 66% → 74% of Q1 revenue
shifts.note: Customer base broadening as AMD gains multiple DC accounts

shifts.trend: R&D +39% to $2.4B Q1 on headcount build; no significant owned capex — fabless model; OpenAI 6 GW deal (Oct 2025) drives demand signal not capex

AMD.verdict: DC growth re-acceleration from +32% to +57% YoY is the single clearest sign AMD is taking share in AI GPU; margin expansion follows naturally. China risk has stepped down from the $800M MI308 charge era to a 25%-tariff friction — manageable. Thesis intact.

AMAT.headline: DRAM/HBM mix rising fast but Q1 headline dipped -2% on weak trailing-edge logic; $253M BIS settlement one-time headwind.

shifts.trend: Revenue +4% FY25 → -2% Q1 FY26 YoY; SemiSys +4% FY25 → -8% Q1 on logic/foundry weakness
shifts.note: Trailing-edge logic demand weak; HBM mix growing but not yet big enough to offset

shifts.trend: DRAM as % of SemiSys: ~19% FY25 → 34% Q1 FY26 (+15pts); NAND held at ~4%; foundry/logic 67% → 58%
shifts.note: HBM technology-transition capex driving DRAM share up materially

shifts.trend: China revenue $8.5B FY25 (-16%) → China $2.1B Q1 (-7% YoY); Taiwan surged +46% Q1 vs +71% FY25
shifts.note: China decline rate moderating; $253M BIS compliance settlement booked Q1

shifts.trend: Gross margin 48.7% FY25 → 49.0% Q1; operating margin -4.3pts Q1 from $253M BIS settlement (one-time)
shifts.note: Underlying gross margin stable; BIS charge distorts Q1 operating profit

shifts.trend: Two customers 19% and 15% FY25; Taiwan jumped to top geography Q1 (+46% YoY); Korea -13% Q1

AMAT.verdict: DRAM/HBM mix rising sharply positions AMAT well for continued HBM capex cycle; near-term revenue soft on logic weakness and the $253M BIS settlement is a one-time item. Story is a bet on DRAM mix shift continuing — not clearly broken, but the wait for logic recovery adds uncertainty.

AMKR.headline: Growth reaccelerating sharply (+27.5% Q1 vs +6.2% FY25); capex ramping dramatically for Arizona; Apple/Qualcomm concentration unchanged.

shifts.trend: Net sales +6.2% FY25 → +27.5% Q1 2026; communications +42%, computing +19%, auto/industrial +28% in Q1
shifts.note: Premium smartphone + datacenter packaging driving broad-based re-acceleration
Arizona 1.8M sq-ft advanced-packaging facility; CHIPS Act $407M funding + 35% ITC

shifts.trend: Gross margin 14.0% FY25 → 14.2% Q1 2026 (+2.3pts YoY); Vietnam ramp headwind FY25 now moderating
shifts.note: Utilization recovery on higher volume driving margin improvement

shifts.trend: Apple 29.8% FY25 / Qualcomm 11.1%; top-10 = 72% — customer mix unchanged

AMKR.verdict: Growth re-acceleration validates the thesis that advanced packaging demand is structurally rising; the 3x capex ramp is the highest-conviction signal management has made. Apple/Qualcomm concentration is a persistent single-name risk but not new.

AMZN.headline: AWS re-accelerating to +28% Q1 vs +20% FY25; capex trajectory doubles YoY; RPO surged to $364B on OpenAI $100B expansion.

shifts.trend: AWS revenue $128.7B FY25 (+20% YoY) → $37.6B Q1 2026 (+28% YoY); Q1 represents $150B run rate
shifts.note: Re-acceleration driven by AI workloads and multi-year cloud contract conversions

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin 35.4% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 2026 — expanding even as capex doubles
shifts.note: Scale economies and pricing discipline maintaining margin despite massive infra investment

shifts.trend: Capex $77.7B FY24 → $128.3B FY25 → $43.2B Q1 2026 alone (tracking >$170B FY26 run rate, +33%+)
shifts.note: Majority for AWS technical infrastructure; 2026 expected to increase further per management

shifts.trend: RPO $244B FY25-end → $364B Q1 2026 (+49% in one quarter); OpenAI expanded existing $38B deal by $100B
shifts.note: RPO concentration in AI hyperscalers; AMZN also invested $50B in OpenAI and $5B+$20B in Anthropic

AMZN.verdict: AWS re-acceleration to +28% while margins expanded is the cleanest proof-point across all hyperscaler prints. The RPO jump to $364B and OpenAI deal expansion confirm the demand signal is structural. Thesis intact and strengthening.

ANET.headline: Revenue re-accelerating to +35% Q1 vs +29% FY25; deferred revenue swelling to $6.2B as AI Ethernet trials convert; GM drifting lower on pricing discounts.

shifts.trend: Revenue +28.6% FY25 → +35.1% Q1 FY26; product revenue +36.6% Q1 on AI Ethernet shipments
shifts.note: Cloud and AI Titans 48% of FY25 revenue; driving the incremental growth

shifts.trend: Memory and silicon supply tightening cited in both filings; ANET procuring ahead with $413M committed inventory
shifts.note: Supply tightness is both an ANET risk (GM pressure) and a signal of demand strength

shifts.trend: Gross margin 64.1% FY25 → 61.9% Q1 FY26 (-2.2pts); large-customer pricing discounts + memory input costs
shifts.note: Structural margin headwind from scale of Cloud Titan deals; not transitory

shifts.trend: Americas 79.7% FY25 → 84.5% Q1; Cloud+AI Titans 48% of FY25; two end-customers 26%+16% in FY25
shifts.note: Heavy US Cloud/AI Titan concentration — single-point risk if deployment pacing slows

shifts.trend: Deferred revenue $2.79B FY24 → $5.37B FY25 → $6.20B Q1 FY26; RPO $7.7B with 91% within 2 years
shifts.note: AI Ethernet acceptance-period contract backlog growing — revenue visibility improving

ANET.verdict: Re-acceleration confirms Cloud Titan AI build is in full swing. Gross margin compression to 61.9% is the new concern — it's structural, not one-time. If that stabilizes around 62%, the thesis holds; if it drops to 58-60%, that's the sign discounting is outrunning the growth story.

AVGO.headline: AI semi revenue tripled in 6 months — $4.1B FY25 Q4 → $10.8B Q2 FY26 (+143% YoY); FY26 AI semi guide raised to $56B; FY27 >$100B reiterated; one distributor 42% of revenue.

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions +22% FY25 → +52% Q1 FY26 → +58% Q2 FY26; AI semi specifically: $4.1B Q4 FY25 → $4.9B Q1 → $10.8B Q2 (+143% YoY); Q3 guided $29.4B total (+84% YoY)
shifts.note: XPU ramp accelerating — $10.8B shipped vs $30B in new bookings in Q2 alone; demand pipeline outrunning deployments

shifts.trend: FY26 AI semi revenue guide: ~$40B (implied FY25 10-K) → $50B (Q1 8-K) → $56B (Q2 8-K); FY27 guide >$100B reiterated Q2
shifts.note: Three consecutive quarterly raises to the same FY guide — management credibility on AI semi demand is unusually high

shifts.trend: One distributor/channel 32% FY25 → 42% Q1 → 42% Q2 (held at peak); top-5 XPU customers ~50%+ of semi revenue; bookings $30B Q2 vs $10.8B shipped implies committed demand >3 quarters ahead
shifts.note: Concentration at 42% is the highest single-name exposure in the tracker; a pause from that one hyperscaler would be -20-25% revenue event

shifts.trend: Gross margin 68% FY25 → 68% Q1 → 77.1% Q2 (+9pts QoQ); semiconductor gross margin expanding as XPU yields improve and VMware software layer re-rates blended mix
shifts.note: Q2 77.1% gross margin is significantly above historical AVGO levels — mix and pricing power both contributing

shifts.trend: Q2 inventory 86 days (building inventory for H2 ramp); $30B bookings vs $10.8B shipped — supply constrained, not demand constrained; management explicitly building ahead for multi-year XPU ramp
shifts.note: Inventory build signals management confidence in H2 demand delivery; $30B bookings level implies sustained hyperscaler commitment

AVGO.verdict: AI semi tripling to $10.8B in a single quarter with $56B FY26 guide and $100B+ FY27 guide is the most aggressive bottleneck thesis print in the tracker. Gross margin at 77.1% shows XPU economics are exceptional. The 42% one-distributor concentration remains the single-name binary risk — but the $30B bookings pipeline means the thesis has 3+ quarters of runway even if that hyperscaler slows. Story fully intact.

CEG.headline: Calpine first full quarter — adj EPS $2.74 (+$0.60 YoY); nuclear at 92.3% capacity factor; PUCT approved CyrusOne Freestone data-center connection; FY26 adj EPS $11-12/share affirmed.

shifts.trend: Pre-Calpine ~26 GW nuclear/gas/storage → post-Calpine 55 GW (21 GW nat gas + nuclear + Geysers geothermal + 800 MW battery); Q1 2026 first full Calpine consolidation quarter — ~$2/share adj EPS accretion realized
shifts.note: Scale-up completed and accretion confirmed in first full quarter; world's largest private power producer

shifts.trend: Q3/FY25: Meta (Clinton nuclear, 20-yr PPA) + Microsoft (Crane/TMI, 20-yr PPA) in place; Q1 2026: PUCT approved new service agreement for CyrusOne data center at Freestone (nat gas) — third hyperscaler signed to Texas gas capacity
shifts.note: Hyperscaler-to-nuclear/gas pipeline now at 3 distinct signed customers — model validating

shifts.trend: Adj operating EPS $8.67 FY24 → $9.39 FY25 → $2.74 Q1 2026 ($10.96 annualized, +17% YoY); FY26 adj EPS guide $11-12/share affirmed Q1; management cited 'hyperscaler capex +75% YoY' as tailwind
shifts.note: Calpine accretion + operational execution driving EPS above FY25 pace already in Q1

shifts.trend: Nuclear fleet capacity factor 92.3% Q1 2026 (record-high operating efficiency); FCF target $8.4B for 2026-27 combined; nuclear output essentially at ceiling — pricing reflects scarcity
shifts.note: Nuclear CF at 92.3% means no further volume upside — pricing is the only lever; power price environment positive for margin expansion

shifts.trend: Calpine acquisition ~$22B (50M shares + $4.5B cash); Crane TMI DOE loan $1B; Q1 financial statements reflect full Calpine debt consolidation; FCF $8.4B 2026-27 target provides 3-4x interest coverage on Calpine debt
shifts.note: Balance sheet complexity elevated but FCF coverage improving as Calpine revenue flows through

CEG.verdict: Calpine accretion confirmed in Q1 ($2.74 adj EPS vs $2.14 Q1 2025), FY26 guide $11-12/share implies ~25% EPS growth YoY, and a third hyperscaler (CyrusOne at Freestone) signed. Nuclear CF at 92.3% means no volume upside — thesis depends on sustained high power prices and continued PPA signings. Story intact and execution on track.

CLS.headline: CCS +76% Q1 vs +42% FY25 as hyperscaler networking switch programs surge; Enterprise recovered +101% after the 2025 AI compute program pause.

shifts.trend: CCS segment +42% FY25 → +76% Q1 FY26; HPS (high-performance switching) +81% FY25 → +63% Q1 (pacing tough comp)
shifts.note: Enterprise end-market swung from -19% FY25 to +101% Q1 as next-gen AI compute program ramped

shifts.trend: FY25: 3 customers each >10% (32%, 14%, 12%), top-10 = 79%; Q1: HPS 42% of revenue (3 hyperscaler customers);
shifts.note: Revenue is still hyper-concentrated in 3 cloud titans — binary risk on program timing

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided ~$1B (6% of revenue vs historical 1.5-2.0%); revolver upsized $750M → $1.75B Q1
shifts.note: Thailand, US, Mexico, Japan capacity expansions for AI/HPS program growth

shifts.trend: CCS segment margin 8.0% Q1 FY25 → 8.6% Q1 FY26; ATS 6.0%; adj EPS $2.16 beat high end of guide
shifts.note: Operating leverage from HPS volumes improving margins

CLS.verdict: Program-driven model means the re-acceleration from +42% to +76% is real but lumpy — the Enterprise +101% recovery shows what happens when a paused program restarts. Story is fully intact; the 6% capex commitment signals management expects the ramp to be durable.

CDNS.headline: Growth re-accelerating from +14% FY25 to +19% Q1; backlog growing to $8B; China re-accelerating post BIS whiplash.

shifts.trend: Revenue +14% FY25 → +19% Q1 FY26; product+maintenance +21% Q1
shifts.note: Geographic breadth: Other Americas +87%, Japan +36%, Other Asia +23% in Q1

shifts.trend: RPO $7.8B FY25-end → $8.0B Q1 FY26; 53% → 55% to recognize in next 12 months
shifts.note: Backlog growing and near-term conversion proportion rising — revenue visibility improving

shifts.trend: China revenue $680M FY25 (+19%) → $189.4M Q1 (+36% YoY, 13% of mix); post BIS-whiplash (May imposed, Jul 2025 rescinded)
shifts.note: BIS 'is-informed' letter resolved; China re-accelerating on 3D991/3E991 product demand

shifts.trend: Operating margin 28% FY25 → 29% Q1; recurring revenue 82% → 77% as hardware/up-front mix grows
shifts.note: FY25 margin had $128.5M BIS/DOJ settlement; Q1 margin cleaner

CDNS.verdict: Thesis intact — growth re-accelerating, backlog growing, China recovering. The BIS whiplash episode is resolved, which removes the regulatory overhang. Main watch item is recurring revenue mix dilution as hardware/up-front component grows.

COHR.headline: D&C segment re-accelerating — +34% Q2 to +41% Q3; gross margin expanding 243bps to 38%; portfolio narrowing to pure AI/DC optics.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +17% Q2 → +21% Q3 YoY; D&C segment +34% Q2 → +41% Q3
shifts.note: Q3 first explicit mention of 'scale across' AI interconnect as new demand category alongside DCI+telecom

shifts.trend: Gross margin 37% (+140bps YoY) Q2 → 38% (+243bps YoY) Q3; 9M gross margin 37%
shifts.note: D&C yield improvements and pricing optimization driving expansion every quarter

shifts.trend: D&C profit +44% Q2 → +49% Q3 YoY; Industrial -10% Q2 → -16% Q3 (divestitures accelerating exit)
shifts.note: Aerospace (Sep 2025) + Munich Germany (Jan 2026) divested — portfolio now near-pure AI optics

shifts.trend: Interest expense -$13M Q3, -$39M YTD on term-loan paydown; $14M Q3 equity-investment gain
shifts.note: Below-the-line improving as debt reduced from divestiture proceeds

COHR.verdict: Thesis strengthening — D&C re-acceleration plus 243bps gross margin expansion in Q3 shows the AI transceiver ramp is structural. The divestiture of industrial businesses has de-risked the portfolio. 'Scale across' interconnect emerging as next catalyst beyond DCI.

CRM.headline: FY26 RPO recovered to $72.4B (+14% YoY) but single-digit revenue growth persists; Agentforce AI monetization still 'relatively new and uncertain'.

shifts.trend: Revenue +9% Q3 FY26 → +10% FY26 full year; single-digit growth consistent across both periods
shifts.note: Agentforce 360 Platform/Slack/Other was the only double-digit segment (+19% Q3)

shifts.trend: Total RPO $59.5B Q3 (down from $63.4B Jan 2025) → $72.4B FY26-end (+14% YoY); cRPO $35.1B (+16%)
shifts.note: cRPO outpacing total RPO signals near-term demand acceleration despite Q3 dip

shifts.trend: Operating margin 19% Q3 → 20% FY26 full year; diluted EPS $7.80 vs $6.36
shifts.note: Margin expansion from restructuring and AI product leverage

shifts.trend: Closed $9.6B Informatica acquisition (Nov 2025) for agentic data management; OCF $15.0B (+15%)
shifts.note: Capital deployed to AI/data cloud platform — returns TBD

CRM.verdict: RPO recovered and cRPO +16% is constructive, but 10% revenue growth is slower than the market for enterprise software. Agentforce AI monetization is the thesis-maker — it's too early to measure, making CRM a 'show me' story for the next 2-3 quarters.

CRWV.headline: RPO surged to $98.8B in one quarter; Microsoft concentration fell from 67% to 45% as Meta emerges at 20%; debt load rising with more capital raised post-quarter.

shifts.trend: Revenue +168% FY25 → +112% Q1 2026 YoY; Q1 revenue $2.08B vs $5.1B FY25 ($8.3B run rate)
shifts.note: High base effect; deceleration on % basis but revenue pace still very strong

shifts.trend: Microsoft 67% of FY25 revenue → 45% Q1 2026; Meta emerging at 20% Q1 (new top-2 customer)
shifts.note: Diversification improving but still hyper-concentrated in 2 customers (65% of Q1)

shifts.trend: Active power 850 MW FY25 (vs 360 MW prior year); RPO $60.7B FY25-end → $98.8B Q1 (63% jump in single quarter)
shifts.note: 3.1 GW contracted power; contract signings outpacing deployments — backlog building

shifts.trend: Total debt $21.6B FY25 → raised $7.8B more post-Q1 ($4.0B converts + $2.8B senior + $1.0B equity)
shifts.note: Debt at effective rates 9-15%; accumulated deficit $3.4B; not yet profitable

CRWV.verdict: RPO at $98.8B is remarkable for a company with a $8B annualized revenue run-rate — the demand pipeline is structurally ahead of capacity. Customer concentration easing (Meta at 20%) is directionally positive. The $21.6B+ debt load at ~10% rates is the thesis risk if build-out costs overrun or a key customer pauses.

CSCO.headline: Networking re-accelerating — +15% Q1 to +21% Q2 on AI Infrastructure; Security declining as Splunk transitions cloud; Silicon One hyperscaler commitments growing.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +8% Q1 → +10% Q2; Networking product category +15% Q1 → +21% Q2 (+$1.4B)
shifts.note: AI Infrastructure plus Service Provider Routing and Data Center Switching as primary drivers

shifts.trend: Security -4% Q2 on Splunk on-prem → cloud shift; Networking +21%; trend expected to continue 2H FY26
shifts.note: Security headwind from business model transition — temporary but multi-quarter

shifts.trend: Silicon One purchase commitments for hyperscalers +10% vs FYE in Q1; continued in Q2; mgmt flags Silicon One commitments add supply-chain exposure
shifts.note: Hyperscalers are driving CSCO to build ahead — both a demand signal and an obsolescence risk

shifts.trend: Product gross margin -0.6pts Q1 on mix/pricing → +0.2pts Q2 on productivity; overall margins stable

CSCO.verdict: Networking re-acceleration to +21% confirms AI Infrastructure and Data Center Switching are real growth levers for CSCO. Security drag is a near-term headwind from the Splunk cloud model transition. Overall thesis intact as a networking infrastructure pick.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog +44% YoY to $14.5B with organic orders +42%; margin compressed -480bps Q1 on tariffs/commodities — the structural vs cyclical question.

shifts.trend: EA backlog $13.2B FY25 (+31% organic) → $14.5B Q1 2026 (+44% YoY); book-to-bill 1.2x consecutive quarters
shifts.note: Demand far outrunning current capacity — delivery lead times still stretched

shifts.trend: EA organic customer orders +42% Q1; EG +20% organic; both segments now citing data center as primary driver
EA operating margin 30.2% FY25 → 25.6% Q1 2026 (-480bps); tariff/commodity inflation ~210bps of headwind
shifts.note: Margin decline is the key risk to the ETN thesis — if pricing can't recover commodity costs, thesis weakens

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal $9.5B acquisition (announced Nov 2025, pending close); completes liquid cooling exposure from chip to grid

ETN.verdict: Orders and backlog are exceptional (+44% YoY backlog). But the 480bps margin compression in Q1 is concerning — if tariff costs prove sticky, margin recovery becomes the key question. Story is a bet on pricing catch-up closing the gap. Boyd Thermal close adds liquid cooling optionality.

FN.headline: Revenue re-accelerating (+35.9% → +39.3%); DCI +90.4% YoY in Q3 as AI inter-datacenter fabric demand surges; Telecom now the largest optical line.

shifts.note: DCI fastest-growing line as AI scale-across inter-DC fabric ramps

shifts.trend: Telecom $412.2M Q2 → $431.4M Q3 (+42.5% YoY); HPC (non-optical) $85.6M Q2 → $106.7M Q3; Datacom essentially flat both quarters
shifts.note: Revenue mix shifting toward higher-value telecom + DCI + HPC; Datacom flat as mix shifts up

shifts.trend: Gross margin 12.2% Q2 → 11.9% Q3 (-30bps QoQ); operating income +52.1% Q3 YoY at 9.9% margin
shifts.note: Thin gross margin profile typical of contract manufacturer; leverage in operating income

shifts.trend: 4 customers each >10% combined 59.1% Q2; same pattern Q3 with Nokia/Infinera + Cisco/Acacia consolidation flagged
shifts.note: Customer consolidation risk as optical industry M&A compresses the buyer count

FN.verdict: DCI +90.4% YoY in Q3 is the clearest proof that AI scale-across inter-DC traffic is a real investment cycle. The contract-manufacturing margin profile means the upside is modest, but volume leverage in operating income is compounding. Thesis intact.

GEV.headline: Total RPO $163.3B (+32% YoY); gas turbine orders 110 units FY25 vs 68 in 2024; Power EBITDA margin expanding from 12.5% to 16.3%.

shifts.trend: Gas turbine GW orders: 20.2 GW FY24 → 29.8 GW FY25 → 8.1 GW Q1 2026 alone (>32 GW annualized pace)
shifts.note: Orders consistently outpacing prior-year; long-duration backlog building

shifts.trend: Power RPO $94.4B FY25 (+29% YoY) → $99.7B Q1 (+30% YoY); total RPO $150.2B → $163.3B
shifts.note: Prolec GE acquisition (Feb 2026) also added to Electrification RPO

shifts.trend: Power EBITDA margin 12.5% FY24 → 14.7% FY25 → 16.3% Q1 2026; FCF $3.7B FY25 → $4.8B Q1 alone
shifts.note: Favorable pricing + higher volume from backlog burn driving margin expansion

shifts.trend: Prolec GE acquisition ~$5.3B (closes Feb 2026); adds 10,000-employee transformer manufacturing JV
shifts.note: Transformer constraint is the bottleneck in US data-center power build — Prolec expands that exposure

GEV.verdict: GEV is one of the cleanest supply-constraint thesis plays in the tracker — gas turbine orders tripled from 2023 to 2025, RPO is at $163B and rising, and Power EBITDA margins are expanding. FCF $4.8B in one quarter. Story fully intact.

GNRC.headline: C&I segment re-accelerating from +4.9% FY25 to +28% Q1 on data center demand; Enercon acquisition adds hyperscale-specific custom power; residential still modest.

shifts.trend: Total revenue -2% FY25 → +12% Q1 FY26; C&I +4.9% FY25 → +28% Q1 on data center customer pull
shifts.note: Large-megawatt C&I product line launched FY25 for data center backup — now delivering

shifts.trend: Residential -6.8% FY25 → +1% Q1 (quiet outage season drag easing); C&I +28% driving recovery
shifts.note: Segment reorganized from Domestic/International to Residential/C&I in Q1 to align with strategy

shifts.trend: Adj EBITDA margin 17.0% FY25 → Q1 Residential 25.1% (vs 20.3%), C&I 13.0% (vs 11.4%); both improving
shifts.note: Enercon acquisition (hyperscale custom power) brings margin accretion

shifts.trend: Large-megawatt backup generator market cited as 'significantly supply-constrained' in FY25 10-K — same commentary implied in Q1 as C&I demand exceeds pace
shifts.note: Supply constraint is a revenue-limiter but also a pricing lever

GNRC.verdict: Data center entry via large-megawatt diesel backup is paying off — C&I +28% Q1 vs +4.9% FY25 is a genuine inflection. Residential seasonality remains a drag but doesn't threaten the DC thesis. Story is still early-stage; execution on the Enercon integration is the next milestone.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud re-accelerating to +63% Q1 vs +36% FY25; op income 3x YoY in one quarter; RPO nearly doubled to $467.6B on multi-GW TPU supply deals.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud +36% FY25 ($58.7B) → +63% Q1 2026 ($20.0B, ~$80B run rate); 3x to 4x acceleration
shifts.note: Multi-GW TPU hardware supply agreements signed in Q1 — first time GCP acting as AI accelerator supplier

shifts.trend: Cloud op income $6.1B FY25 → $6.6B Q1 alone (~$26B run rate); margin 33% Q1 vs 23.7% FY25
shifts.note: Margin expanding dramatically even as capex doubles — scale operating leverage

shifts.trend: Capex $52.5B FY24 → $91.4B FY25 → $35.7B Q1 2026 alone (tracking >$140B FY26 run rate +55%+)
shifts.note: 2026 capex guidance: 'significantly increase relative to 2025' — consistent across both filings

shifts.trend: Revenue backlog $242.8B FY25-end → $467.6B Q1 2026 (93% increase in one quarter, driven by cloud contracts)
shifts.note: TPU multi-GW supply deals represent a new monetization of compute hardware via GCP

GOOGL.verdict: Google Cloud +63% with 33% operating margin is the best single-quarter showing from any hyperscaler in the tracker. The $467.6B RPO (nearly doubled in a quarter) confirms the demand pipeline is structural and accelerating. Thesis very much intact.

HPE.headline: Networking +151% Q1 on full-quarter Juniper inclusion; Cloud+AI server revenue declining; memory component shortages flagged as new gross-margin risk.

shifts.trend: Networking +51.1% FY25 (Juniper contributed $2.1B, partial quarter) → +151.5% Q1 FY26 (full-quarter inclusion)
shifts.note: Apples-to-oranges compare since Juniper closed 7/2/25; organic like-for-like YoY won't be clean until FY26 Q3

shifts.trend: Server (renamed Cloud+AI) segment revenue -2.7% Q1; AI servers growing but GPU-heavy mix carries 'limited margins'
shifts.note: AI server growth is margin-dilutive — a structural headwind on the server segment

shifts.trend: Gross margin 35.9% Q1 (+670bps YoY) on Juniper Networking revenue mix; non-GAAP operating margin +280bps
shifts.note: Memory component shortages flagged as new risk to gross margin going forward

shifts.trend: $600M Juniper synergies by FY28 requiring $800M investment; $350M Catalyst cost program active; $275M charges in FY25
shifts.note: Synergy capture is multi-year; near-term opex elevated from integration

HPE.verdict: Juniper integration is clearly margin-accretive (+670bps gross margin Q1). But Cloud+AI server revenue declining and AI server margins 'limited' — HPE is benefiting from the networking thesis but is challenged in the compute server segment. Memory shortage headwind is new risk to watch.

HUBB.headline: Electrical Solutions organic growth re-accelerating from +7.1% FY25 to +10.6% Q1 on datacenter strength; Utility Solutions solid on T&D market.

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions +7.1% FY25 → +10.6% organic Q1; Utility Solutions steady at ~6-7% organic
shifts.note: Datacenter vertical consistently cited as primary volume driver in both filings

shifts.trend: Overall adjusted operating margin +110bps Q1; Electrical Solutions adjusted margin -30bps Q1 (commodity/tariff offset by price/volume); Utility Solutions +190bps
shifts.note: Tariff headwind partly offset by pricing — manageable

shifts.trend: $958M acquisitions in FY25 to extend product reach; backlog $2.16B FY25 (+14%)
shifts.note: Acquisition-led expansion strategy continues

HUBB.verdict: Steady, reliable data-center-adjacent story. Organic growth re-accelerating to +10.6% with manageable margin pressure. HUBB doesn't have the backlog visibility of ETN or PWR, but the business is executing consistently.

INTC.headline: DCAI +22% Q1 on demand exceeding supply; Intel Foundry losses not improving; supply constraints expected through H1 2026 on substrates and memory.

shifts.trend: DCAI +$794M FY25 (+7% YoY) → DCAI +22% Q1 2026 on premium mix and demand-based pricing; volume -5% supply-constrained
shifts.note: Demand actually exceeds supply — Q1 revenue limited by Intel 7 constraints, not demand

shifts.trend: Intel 7 supply constraints 'most severe in Q1 2026' per FY25 10-K → Q1 10-Q confirms 'persist at least through H1 2026' on substrates, memory, components
shifts.note: Supply is the binding constraint on both server (DCAI) and client (CCG) revenue growth

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry operating loss $10.3B FY25 → $2.4B Q1 2026 ($9.6B annualized) — not improving; external revenue $174M Q1 (vs $307M FY25 full year, flat trend)
shifts.note: 14A risk: may pause or discontinue if no significant external foundry customer — still unresolved

shifts.trend: CCG ASPs +16% Q1 on premium mix; DCAI ASPs +27% on premium mix; gross margin 39.4% Q1 (+2.5pts YoY)
shifts.note: ASP improvement from mix/pricing disciplines offsetting volume weakness

shifts.trend: FY25: US Gov warrant (159M shares at $20), SoftBank $2B (87M shares at $23), NVIDIA $5B strategic investment; Apollo SCIP buyout completes Q2 2026
shifts.note: Significant external equity injection signals strategic importance but dilutes existing shareholders

INTC.verdict: Demand-side story is real (DCAI +22%) but supply-constrained through H1 2026. Intel Foundry losses at $9.6B annualized pace show no sign of inflecting; the 14A decision remains the existential question. NVIDIA $5B investment signals long-term strategic value but Intel's path to external foundry scale is still uncertain.

KLAC.headline: Revenue re-accelerating from +7% to +11% YoY; Korea exploded +80% on HBM; China declining share from 30% to 24%; DRAM/HBM now primary growth engine.

shifts.note: Memory customer DRAM led by HBM is the dominant driver in both quarters

shifts.trend: China 30.2% of Q2 revenue ($995M) → 24% of Q3 revenue ($830M); nine-month China flat at $3.09B as export controls constrain
shifts.note: China share declined 6pts in one quarter — export controls materially limiting the upside

shifts.trend: Memory DRAM/HBM cited as primary growth driver in both quarters; advanced packaging adoption growing; foundry/logic steady
shifts.note: Services +18% Q2, +16% Q3 — installed base growing supports recurring revenue

shifts.trend: Gross margin 61.4% Q2 → 61.1% Q3 (-0.3pts); DRAM chip costs for image computers cited as transitory headwind
shifts.note: Management calls it transitory; record quarter in Q3 suggests pricing power intact

shifts.trend: 16th → 17th consecutive annual dividend increase; Q3 buybacks $626M; $7B added to repurchase authorization
shifts.note: Capital return cadence consistent and accelerating

KLAC.verdict: Two-quarter story of DRAM/HBM driving the acceleration and China declining as a share of revenue — China risk diversifying into Korea/Taiwan/NA. Gross margin headwind from image-computer DRAM costs looks manageable. Thesis intact.

LEU.headline: Backlog $3.9B (+$0.1B from YE); $900M DOE HALEU task order won Jan 2026; Piketon EPC signed with Fluor; SWU spot price $200 vs $34 historic low.

shifts.trend: Q1 revenue $76.7M (+5%); LEU $44.6M (-13%, volume -47% offset by avg price +52%); Technical Solutions $32.1M (+47%)
shifts.note: Volume dip is timing of SWU deliveries; average price +52% shows pricing power

shifts.trend: SWU spot price $200 at Q1-end (vs $34 historic low in 2018, +488%); non-Russian market undersupplied; Russian supply 43% of global enrichment capacity
shifts.note: Spot price did not budge even with Q1 volume softness — structural supply shortage

shifts.trend: FY25: $900M DOE HALEU task order won Jan 5; Feb 2026 signed Fluor EPC contract for Piketon LEU+HALEU expansion; Dec 2025 Oak Ridge centrifuge manufacturing started ($560M+ multi-year)
shifts.note: First new US centrifuges expected operational Ohio 2029

shifts.trend: Backlog $3.8B FY25-end → $3.9B Q1; LEU $3.1B ($2.4B contingent under definitive agreements pending capacity); Technical Solutions $0.9B
shifts.note: March 2026: exploring JV with Oklo on HALEU deconversion at Piketon — new collaboration

LEU.verdict: SWU spot at $200 (12x the 2018 low) is the simplest signal of how tight nuclear fuel supply is. Centrus is uniquely positioned as the only US company with NRC approval to produce HALEU. DOE funding risk (FY27 budget excluding cascade) is the key watch item.

LITE.headline: Revenue nearly doubled YoY in Q3 (+90.1%); gross margin +1,540bps to 44.2%; demand allocation language hardened — supply rationing InP/EML laser chips for 1.6T transceivers.

shifts.note: Cloud transceivers (Systems) +121.1% Q3; laser chip ramp driving Components +77.3%

shifts.trend: Gross margin 36.1% Q2 (+1,130bps YoY) → 44.2% Q3 (+1,540bps YoY); 9M gross margin 38.8% (+1,300bps)
shifts.note: 200G-lane mix shift at 1.6T cycle structurally re-rating LITE gross margins

shifts.trend: Q2: 'demand is outpacing current supply which has led to decisions on supply allocation' → Q3 language hardened: 'has required us to make decisions on supply allocation'
shifts.note: LITE is the InP/EML supply choke point for 1.6T transceivers — allocation language strengthening confirms structural bottleneck

shifts.trend: Q2: 2 customers at 24% and 17% (combined 41%) → Q3: top customer rose to 26% (combined 38% for top 2)
shifts.note: Top customer share rising — likely one AI hyperscaler driving optical circuit switch + transceiver combined orders

shifts.trend: Optical circuit switches >$10M Q2 → >$25M Q3 → >$38M 9M; initial ramp phase confirmed delivering revenue
shifts.note: OCS is a new high-margin product line entering volume production

LITE.verdict: 90% revenue growth + 1,540bps gross margin expansion in a single quarter with hardening supply allocation language is the strongest bottleneck signal in the optics layer. LITE is the InP laser choke point for 1.6T — thesis is not just intact, it's proving out in real-time.

LRCX.headline: Nine-month revenue +24% YoY; Memory mix rising to 39% as DRAM investment surges; Foundry slipping to 54%; China stable at ~34%.

shifts.trend: Revenue $5.34B Q2 → $5.84B Q3 (+9% QoQ); nine-month revenue $16.51B vs $13.26B prior year (+24%)
shifts.note: Consistent 24%+ YoY growth across the nine-month window

shifts.trend: Memory mix: 34% Q2 → 39% Q3; Foundry: 59% Q2 → 54% Q3; DRAM/NVM investment strengthening both periods
shifts.note: DRAM including HBM investment is the rising share — foundry mature-node declining

shifts.trend: China 35% Q2 → 34% Q3; Korea 20% → 23%; Taiwan 20% → 23%; China share modestly declining
shifts.note: Geographic diversification gradually reducing China concentration

shifts.trend: Gross margin 49.6% Q2 → 49.8% Q3 (+0.2pts); essentially flat; better factory efficiency offset by mix
Q3 capital returns $1.16B repurchases + $326M dividends + $751M debt paydown; cash dropped to $4.77B from $6.20B
shifts.note: Aggressive return of capital alongside debt paydown

LRCX.verdict: Steady compounder with DRAM/HBM mix rising structurally. China at 34% remains a moderate export-control tail risk. Gross margin stability at ~49-50% is the quality signal. Story intact.

META.headline: Revenue re-accelerating to +33% Q1 vs +22% FY25; 2026 capex guidance raised from $115-135B to $125-145B; commitments now $237.7B+ non-cancelable.

shifts.trend: Revenue $201.0B FY25 (+22% YoY) → $56.3B Q1 2026 (+33% YoY); ad impressions +19%, price per ad +12%
shifts.note: Advertising monetization improving — AI Recommendations driving engagement and pricing

shifts.trend: Capex $72.2B FY25 → FY26 guided $115-135B at year-end → RAISED to $125-145B in Q1 ($10B increase at both ends)
shifts.note: $19.8B Q1 alone; off-balance-sheet leases not yet commenced = $182.9B — total commitment dwarfs disclosed capex

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments $237.7B Q1 ($42.3B due 2026 + $47.7B in 2027); added $24B more in April 2026
shifts.note: META is effectively the largest single buyer of compute/power capacity in the tracker

shifts.trend: Reality Labs operating loss $19.2B FY25; Q1 RL loss $4.03B; similar losses expected FY26 per management
shifts.note: RL is a persistent ~$19B/year drag — not worsening but not improving

META.verdict: Revenue re-acceleration to +33% with capex raised again to $125-145B is the clearest signal META is betting big on AI infrastructure. The $237.7B non-cancelable commitment makes META the single largest demand signal for data center supply chain. Story very much intact.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center sales +$221.7M nine-month YoY; segment +51% Q3 vs +24% Q2; Reverse Morris Trust spin announced to become pure-play data-center cooling company.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions +24% Q2 → +51% Q3; data center product sales: +$67.4M Q2 → 9M total +$221.7M YoY
shifts.note: Hyperscale/colocation in North America and Europe cited as primary demand driver in both quarters

shifts.trend: Jan 2026: Reverse Morris Trust announced — Performance Technologies spun/merged with Gentherm; MOD retains Climate Solutions as pure-play data center + commercial HVAC
shifts.note: Portfolio simplification accelerates; estimated $1.0B transaction value; closes end of CY26

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions margin 13.7% Q2 → 15.3% Q3 (still below historical ~17-18%); ramp inefficiencies abating but not resolved
shifts.note: Margin recovery is the key execution watch item post-spin

shifts.trend: Q2: announced $100M incremental data center manufacturing capex; Q3: incremental data center manufacturing lease signed
shifts.note: US capacity expansion ongoing; funded via revolver + operating cash flow

MOD.verdict: Pure-play data center cooling thesis clarified by the Gentherm RMT. Climate Solutions re-accelerating strongly; margin is the execution risk. Becoming a pure-play DC cooling company is structurally positive positioning.

MSFT.headline: Azure re-accelerating from +39% Q2 to +40% Q3; commercial RPO stable at $625-627B; quarterly capex steady at $29-31B.

shifts.trend: Azure +39% Q2 → +40% Q3; Microsoft Cloud +26% Q2 ($51.5B) → +29% Q3 ($54.5B)
shifts.note: Consistent re-acceleration in Azure; slight uptick from Q2 to Q3

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO $625B Q2 → $627B Q3 — essentially flat quarter-over-quarter; still +99-110% YoY
shifts.note: RPO not growing quarter-over-quarter suggests contract signings pacing with revenue recognition

shifts.trend: Additions to property/equipment $29.9B Q2 → $30.9B Q3; 9M total $80.1B (vs $40.9B prior year, +96%)
shifts.note: Per-quarter capex remarkably stable — consistent $30B/quarter run rate

shifts.trend: Microsoft Cloud gross margin 67% (cited Q2) on AI infrastructure investments; non-GAAP operating margins stable
shifts.note: Cloud GM declining from higher levels as AI infra depreciation increases

MSFT.verdict: Azure at +40% YoY with $30B/quarter capex is a consistent execution story. Commercial RPO stability at $627B means the backlog is holding — not yet growing quarter-over-quarter, worth monitoring. Story intact.

MRVL.headline: Data Center +46% FY26 (74% of revenue); FY25 restructuring impairments now behind — gross margin 51% vs 41% prior year; Celestial AI + XConn acquisitions close.

shifts.trend: Data Center +38% Q3 FY26 → +46% FY26 full year; total revenue +37% Q3 → +42% FY26
shifts.note: AI custom ASIC and electro-optics/silicon photonics are the growth drivers in both periods

shifts.trend: Gross margin 51.6% Q3 FY26 → 51.0% FY26 (vs 41.3% FY25 — prior year had $358M impairments)
shifts.note: Underlying margin improvement; prior year impairments make Y/Y comparison misleading

shifts.trend: Direct Customer A 13% Q3 → 14% FY26; Distributor A 38% Q3 → 37% FY26; top-10 ~81% of revenue
shifts.note: Customer concentration remarkably stable across both periods; distributor acts as hyperscaler conduit

shifts.trend: Post-year-end: Celestial AI ~$1.3B cash + 24.5M shares (photonic fabric); XConn $280M + 2.1M shares (PCIe/CXL switching)
shifts.note: MRVL assembling full custom-silicon + CPO + CXL stack through acquisitions

shifts.trend: Data center as % of revenue: 73% Q3 → 74% FY26; Communications consolidated from 4 sub-markets into 1 in FY26 Q4
shifts.note: Segment simplification mirrors strategy — data center is the only high-growth business

MRVL.verdict: Data Center at +46% with 74% revenue share and impairments now behind confirms the MRVL pivot thesis worked. Celestial AI + XConn acquisitions extend the stack into photonic fabric and CXL — ambitious roadmap from 5nm to 1.4nm. Concentration (Distributor A at 37%) remains the main risk.

MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

MXL.headline: Revenue re-accelerating from +30% FY25 to +43% Q1 on Infrastructure optical DSP and broadband; customer concentration improving as top-10 dropped from 65% to 56%.

shifts.trend: Revenue +30% FY25 ($467.6M) → +43% Q1 2026 ($137.2M); Infrastructure +$36.3M Q1 (optical, wireless backhaul/access)
shifts.note: 800G to 1.6T optical DSP cycle as explicitly cited growth driver in both filings

shifts.trend: Top customer >20% FY24 → 13% Q1 2026; top-10 customers 65% FY25 → 56% Q1
shifts.note: Broader customer base emerging as infrastructure optical products gain multiple design-wins

shifts.trend: Broadband +59% FY25 (+$87.6M); Infrastructure +$34.3M FY25, +$36.3M Q1 — both positive; Industrial/multi-market recovering (-$37M FY25 → +$3.9M Q1)
shifts.note: Industrial cyclical recovery adding to structural optical/broadband growth

shifts.trend: Gross margin 57% FY25 (+3pts on mix and lower intangible amortization); Q1 gross margin not separately disclosed but positive mix

MXL.verdict: MXL is a small-cap pure-play on the 800G→1.6T optical DSP cycle with improving customer diversification. +43% Q1 growth on top of +30% FY25 shows the cycle is still in early innings. The key risk is customer concentration still at 56% in top-10 and lumpy quarterly revenue mix.

NOW.headline: Consistent 21-22% revenue growth with stable RPO (+25-27%); Now Assist AI agent layer deepening across all product lines.

shifts.trend: Revenue +21% FY25 ($13.28B) → +22% Q1 2026 ($3.77B); subscription revenue +21%/+22% in both periods
shifts.note: Remarkably consistent growth trajectory — no acceleration or deceleration signal
Slight cRPO moderation to +23% from +25%; worth watching for two more quarters

shifts.trend: Self-hosted (US Federal, on-prem) $83M FY25 → $138M Q1 2026 vs $157M Q1 2025 (slight decline); AI product footprint expanding across ITSM/CSM/HRSD/SecOps
shifts.note: Self-hosted upfront decline Q1 is timing, not structural

shifts.trend: Subscription gross margin consistent; self-hosted mix slightly pressures margin; OBBBA domestic R&D expensing benefit minimal

NOW.verdict: Textbook consistency — 21-22% revenue growth, RPO growing 25-27%, Now Assist deepening AI product footprint. cRPO slight moderation to +23% is the only early warning worth watching. Story intact.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection +76.1% Q1 vs +42.2% FY25 on EPG acquisition + organic +50.1%; segment margin expanding to 22.7%.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +29.5% FY25 → +53.5% Q1 FY26; Systems Protection +42.2% FY25 → +76.1% Q1
shifts.note: EPG ($1.0B acquisition, closed May 2025) contributed $121M Q1 on top of 46% organic from infrastructure/data center

shifts.trend: Systems Protection segment margin 20.7% FY25 → 22.7% Q1 2026 (+200bps) on volume leverage
shifts.note: Gross margin -290bps Q1 on tariffs/raw materials, offset by segment operating leverage

shifts.trend: Divested Thermal Management to Brookfield for $1.6B net (Jan 2025); acquired EPG for $1.0B (May 2025); $253M FY25 repurchases + dividends
shifts.note: Portfolio simplification toward enclosures + liquid cooling — Thermal Management divested, EPG added

shifts.trend: Backlog $2.3B at YE25 (EPG + organic data center growth); firm orders expected to ship in 2026
shifts.note: Data center cited as primary demand driver across both periods

NVT.verdict: EPG acquisition accretion plus organic data-center growth driving strong acceleration. Systems Protection segment margin expanding even with tariff headwinds on gross margin — operating leverage winning. Thesis intact.

OKLO.headline: Meta prepayment agreement signed Jan 2026 for 1.2 GW Pike County campus — first hyperscaler to prepay for advanced fission capacity; 2028 first commercial deployment target unchanged.

shifts.trend: Q3 FY25: order book 18,100 MWe (12 GW Switch + 4,750 MWe new LOIs); FY25 10-K: Meta 1.2 GW prepayment added + LOIs with Equinix, Diamondback, Prometheus Hyperscale active
shifts.note: First hyperscaler (Meta) moving from LOI to prepayment agreement — stronger commitment signal

shifts.trend: FY26 cash burn guided $80-100M operating + $350-450M investing; FY25 opex $139.3M (+164%); raised $300M ATM Q4 2025
shifts.note: Tennessee Advanced Fuel Center up to $1.68B investment with Kiewit as lead constructor

shifts.trend: Q3: NRC Phase I pre-application completed with no significant gaps; PDC topical report accepted; 3 DOE Reactor Pilot projects selected; Feb 2026: Aurora Nuclear Safety Design Agreement DOE approved
shifts.note: Regulatory milestones clearing consistently on accelerated timeline

shifts.trend: Cash burn $48.7M 9M FY25; accumulated deficit $199.3M at FY25-end; still pre-revenue; $1.68B Tennessee fuel center adds to commitment stack
shifts.note: Pre-revenue; investment thesis is entirely forward-looking on 2028 first commercial deployment

OKLO.verdict: Meta prepayment is the qualitative breakthrough — converts the order book from LOIs to actual hyperscaler money committed. Regulatory milestones clearing ahead of schedule. The investment thesis requires 2028 commercial deployment; any schedule slip is the key de-risking event to watch.

ORCL.headline: OCI re-accelerating from +68% Q2 to +84% Q3; RPO $552.6B vs $130.2B YoY; 9M capex $39.2B — 3x prior year run rate.

shifts.trend: Cloud infrastructure +68% Q2 ($4.08B) → +84% Q3 ($4.89B, +81% constant currency); total revenue +14% Q2 → +22% Q3
shifts.note: AI workload demand driving OCI revenue re-acceleration

shifts.trend: RPO $523.3B Q2 (vs $97.3B YoY) → $552.6B Q3 (vs $130.2B YoY) — 4-5x YoY increase; both quarters driven by major cloud contract signings
shifts.note: RPO growing quarter-over-quarter — demand pipeline still building, not harvesting

shifts.trend: H1 capex $20.5B → 9M capex $39.2B (3x vs 9M prior year); trend expected to continue 'next few fiscal years'
shifts.note: Issued $42.7B total senior notes (Sept 2025 + Feb 2026) to fund build-out

shifts.trend: Cloud and software expenses +49% 9M as infra spend ramps; operating margin declining as capex scales
shifts.note: Margin compression expected to persist during build-out phase

ORCL.verdict: OCI +84% with $552.6B RPO is exceptional — ORCL is executing the largest capex ramp in its history. The issuance of $42.7B in senior notes signals management conviction. Margin compression during build is expected and acceptable if OCI maintains 80%+ growth trajectory.

PLTR.headline: Revenue accelerated from +53% FY25 to +85% Q1; commercial outgrowing government at +95%; operating margin expanded from 31% to 46%.

shifts.trend: Revenue $4.5B FY25 (+53% YoY) → $1.63B Q1 2026 (+85% YoY, ~$6.5B run rate)
shifts.note: Commercial +95% outpacing government +76% — commercial AI enterprise adoption accelerating

shifts.trend: Income from ops $1.41B FY25 (31% margin) → $754M Q1 2026 (46% margin); op margin +15pts YoY
shifts.note: Operating leverage exceptional — revenue growing faster than costs on high-margin software

shifts.trend: Commercial contribution margin 60% FY25 → 73% Q1; Gov contribution 66% FY25 → 73% Q1 — both segments expanding
shifts.note: Gross margin 87% Q1 — software-only gross margin profile confirming

shifts.trend: Gov 54% / commercial 46% FY25 → gov 52.5% / commercial 47.5% Q1; no customer >10% in either period
shifts.note: Customer base diversifying as commercial bootcamp strategy scales

PLTR.verdict: +85% Q1 growth with 46% operating margins and commercial outpacing government is the cleanest growth-quality combination in enterprise software in the tracker. AIP/Foundry platform monetization is clearly working. Story fully intact and accelerating.

PWR.headline: Backlog $48.47B at Q1-end (+$4.5B in a single quarter); RPO $26.24B; Q1 revenue +26.3%; operating margins expanding YoY.

shifts.trend: Revenue +20.3% FY25 → +26.3% Q1 2026; Electric segment added $1.52B Q1 YoY
shifts.note: Data center demand driving both Electric and Underground & Infrastructure segments via CEI and DSI acquisitions

shifts.trend: Backlog $43.98B FY25-end (+27.3% YoY) → $48.47B Q1 (+10.2% QoQ in single quarter); RPO $26.24B (+10.4% QoQ)
shifts.note: Backlog growing sequentially faster than revenue — demand outpacing capacity

shifts.trend: Q1 operating margin 4.3% vs 3.8% prior year; gross margin 14.1% vs 13.4%; net income +55%
shifts.note: Data center work higher-margin than legacy utility work — mix improvement

shifts.trend: $3.30B deployed on acquisitions in 2025 (DSI mechanical + CEI electrical); Q1 SG&A +$127M from acquisition integrations
shifts.note: Acquisition-led expansion continuing to build technical DC capability

PWR.verdict: Sequential backlog growth of $4.5B in a single quarter is compelling — demand for electrical infrastructure is clearly outrunning supply of qualified contractors. PWR is a direct beneficiary of data center power build, with improving margins confirming mix quality. Thesis fully intact.

SNOW.headline: Revenue growth consistent at +29-31%; RPO grew to $9.8B with 46% near-term conversion; NRR 125% for both periods — stable but slight moderation from 126%.

shifts.trend: Product revenue +29% Q3 FY26 ($1.16B) → +31% FY26 implied; revenue $1.21B total Q3 → $4.7B FY26 total
shifts.note: AI and data workload consumption holding steady; no acceleration signal yet
Customer count and RPO both growing; $1M+ customers 576 Q3 → 733 FY26 (+27%)

shifts.trend: NRR 125% both periods (down from 126% one year prior); consumption model means NRR expected to decline long-term as base matures
shifts.note: NRR still comfortably above 120%; management expects gradual moderation as install base grows

shifts.trend: Net loss $1.3B FY26; SBC ~34% of revenue (down from 41%); OCF $1.2B positive
shifts.note: SBC dilution declining; operating cash flow positive — financial discipline improving

SNOW.verdict: Steady compounder executing consistently. NRR at 125% with customer count +21% and $1M+ accounts +27% shows healthy consumption growth. No major inflection signal in either direction — this is a 'hold thesis intact' read.

SNPS.headline: Ansys ($756.6M contribution FY25, $885.6M Q1) creating inorganic revenue jump but Design IP remains weak; $13.5B debt load being paid down aggressively.

shifts.trend: Revenue +15% FY25 ($7.05B, $756.6M from Ansys) → +66% Q1 FY26 ($2.41B, $885.6M from Ansys); organic core EDA +6-7% in both periods
shifts.note: Inorganic Ansys contribution dominates reported growth; organic EDA growth mid-single-digits

shifts.trend: Design IP -22% in FY25 ex-Ansys (China weakness + BIS + roadmap issues) → -6% Q1 FY26; muted Design IP growth expected all FY26
shifts.note: BIS whiplash (May imposed/Jul rescinded) hit IP product design wins in Q3 FY25; multi-quarter recovery

shifts.trend: $13.5B total debt FY25-end (Ansys-related) → paid off $3.5B term loan in Q1 FY26; buying back shares suspended pending debt paydown; share buyback to resume when leverage is reduced
shifts.note: Aggressive debt reduction while suspending buybacks — right priority sequencing

shifts.trend: Operating income $914.9M FY25 (compressed by $457.8M Ansys amortization) → $203M Q1 FY26 ($394.1M amortization Q1)
shifts.note: Amortization headwind = ~$1.6B/year at current pace — margin understates cash earnings

shifts.trend: $300-350M restructuring program ('2026 Plan') for post-Ansys workforce reduction; $118.3M charged Q1; Processor IP divested to GlobalFoundries
shifts.note: Integration costs winding down; portfolio pruning focusing on highest-growth EDA/simulation

SNPS.verdict: Integration thesis is playing out — Ansys contributing $885M+ per quarter and backlog stable at $11.3B. Design IP weakness is real but contained. The key signal to watch is when debt drops enough to resume buybacks and GAAP margins recover from amortization drag.

VRT.headline: Americas +53% Q1 vs +42% FY25; backlog more than doubled YoY to $15B at YE25; gross margin expanded 400bps to 37.7% despite tariffs.

shifts.trend: Total revenue +27.7% FY25 → +30.1% Q1 FY26; Americas +41.9% FY25 → +53.1% Q1
shifts.note: AI/HPC demand cited explicitly; hybrid air+liquid cooling + HVDC architectures as key demand drivers

shifts.trend: Backlog $7.2B YE24 → $15.0B YE25 (more than doubled); Q1 management says 'very robust' demand
shifts.note: Backlog more than doubled in FY25 — demand structurally ahead of production capacity

shifts.trend: Gross margin 36.3% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 2026 (+400bps YoY); Americas op margin 26.8% FY25 → 27.0% Q1
shifts.note: Pricing power overcoming tariff headwinds — operating leverage from volume

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $226.4M → FY26 guided $425-525M (2x); reaffirmed Q1; PurgeRite acquired Q1
shifts.note: CY26 capex doubling to support global capacity expansion across Americas/APAC/EMEA

VRT.verdict: Americas +53% with 27% operating margin and backlog at $15B+ is an exceptional quality print. Gross margin expansion despite tariffs shows pricing power at the bottleneck. FY26 capex doubling confirms management conviction. Thesis is very intact — VRT is the clearest cooling infrastructure bottleneck play.
